name: Eekso — The Top Floor
dmNotes:
THE SAFE(ISH) FLOOR — this is the exhale and the clue drop. Two jobs: (1) give them the journal that warns about what's below, so descending is a choice they make with their eyes open; (2) the mimic feast, which is the one real threat up here.

The umber hulk is BACKSTORY, not an encounter — it ate these tunnels out long ago and is gone. The broken walls should read as wrong before anyone explains them. If the party gets paranoid hunting a hulk that isn't here, that's free tension; let them sweep.

The captain's quarters are deliberately empty of treasure. Many owners, all stripped. Resist the urge to reward the search — the reward on this floor is INFORMATION.
scriptText:
# The Top Floor

The stair comes out into open cave.

That is the first strange thing — this is the *top* of the fortress, the floor a commander would have taken for himself, and most of it isn't built at all. It's a vast rough cavern with a packed dirt floor, and the finished rooms cling to one corner of it like something left over.

The air is dry and moves a little. After the floors below, it almost feels clean.

> [!dm] This floor is the party's breather. Let them slow down. Let them consider a rest.
>
> The threat here is exactly one thing (the mimics), and the payoff is exactly one thing (the journal). Everything else is texture and empty rooms.

## The Dead Fountain

{{music:ruin}} Where the built section starts, there's a small court that somebody once meant as a pleasant place.

A round stone fountain sits at the middle of it, bone dry, its basin full of grey dust. Around it, in beds cut into the floor, are trees — long dead, bare, still standing because nothing up here has enough weather to knock them over.

Somebody carried soil up here for these. Somebody watered them by hand.

- The basin has a residue ring — it held water for years, then didn't
- One of the dead trees has initials carved in it, weathered nearly smooth
- A child's-height handprint in the old plaster of the wall, fired hard by time

## The Broken Walls

{{music:wrong}} Past the court, the walls stop making sense.

There are holes in them. Not doorways, not collapses — *holes*, roughly round, six feet across, punched clean through two feet of dressed stone. One wall has three in a row. The rubble lies inside the rooms, which means whatever made them came **through** from the cavern.

And the edges are wrong. They aren't cracked or shattered. They're scooped — like the stone was taken out in bites.

> [!dm] **This is an umber hulk's work, and it is LONG gone.** It ate these tunnels out of the rock generations ago and moved on.
>
> - **Investigation DC 13:** the breaks are old — dust has settled in them, and one has a dead tree root growing through it
> - **Nature / Dungeoneering DC 15:** the cavern itself is a hulk-warren. Most of this floor's open space was *eaten*.
> - The party fought a hulk two sessions ago. They will recognise this. **Let them get scared and sweep the room.** There is nothing to find. That's the gift.

## The Captain's Quarters

Through the largest hole: what were once somebody's private rooms.

A bed frame with no bed. A desk with its drawers pulled out and stacked neatly on the floor by looters who had plenty of time. A fireplace full of cold ash and, in it, the corner of a burnt ledger.

Every hook on the walls is empty. Every shelf is bare. Nothing valuable has survived here in a very long time, because a great many people have already looked.

> [!dm] **Deliberately empty.** Many owners, all stripped. If they search hard, the reward is atmosphere, not loot:
> - The burnt ledger's surviving corner lists supply counts. The numbers get smaller each month, then stop.
> - Names scratched inside a cupboard door — a roster, crossed out one by one in different hands
> - A good boot. Just the one.

## The Campsite — The Journal

{{music:safe}} {{amb:nox-campfire-small|vol=30%|in=3s}} Tucked in the southwest, behind a spur of rock that hides it from the open cave, there's a camp — and this one is *recent*.

Weeks, not decades. A fire ring with charcoal still black. Bedding. A pack, still packed.

Whoever made this was hiding, and was careful, and did not come back for their things.

> [!dm] **THE CLUE DROP.** This is why the floor exists. Hand them the journal and let them read it aloud.
>
> Write it in the voice of a scared, practical person — a scavenger, not a scholar. The tiers, cut to taste:
> - We came in for the fittings. Good stone, good iron, nobody home. Easy work.
> - Don't walk on the rugs. Karst is gone. We heard him for a while. Then we didn't.
> - There is something living down there and it does not eat what it takes. It *keeps* it.
> - Do not follow the blood. The blood is going somewhere.
> - Last entry, different hand, hurried: **It knows how many of us there are.**
>
> This is also the natural **long rest** spot if they want one. It is genuinely safe. Let them have it — they'll need it.

## The Feast

{{music:mimic}} East of the camp, past a hanging curtain that someone strung across a gap in the rock, there is a table.

It is laid for a meal. Roast fowl, bread, fruit, a jug — laid out neat and steaming, in a cave, a hundred miles from a kitchen, in a fortress where nothing has eaten in forty years.

It smells *wonderful*.

> [!dm] **Two mimics.** The table and the food are the same creature — the 'table' and the 'roast' are one body each.
>
> - **Perception DC 16 / Arcana DC 14:** the steam rises but never disperses. The shadows under the table don't match the light.
> - **Trigger:** touching the food, sitting, or reaching for the jug. If they hang back, the curtain behind them *stirs* — cut off the retreat.
> - Adhesive grab. Whoever reaches first is stuck to a mouth.
> - Nothing about this room is a puzzle. It's a bite for the greedy and a scare for everyone else.
>
> After: they can take real supplies from the mimics' hoard — coins and metal, the indigestible leavings of everyone who fell for it.

ideas.text: Someone hauled soil up here and hand-watered trees in a lightless cave — the most human thing in Eekso, and it's dead
The wall holes are scooped, not shattered — the stone was taken out in bites
Party fought an umber hulk two sessions ago; let them sweep for one that isn't here. Free tension.
A roster scratched inside a cupboard door, names crossed out one at a time in different handwriting
Journal's last line, different hand: 'It knows how many of us there are.'
The mimic feast steams, but the steam never disperses

entities.name: The scavenger's journal
entities.note: Weeks old. Warns about the rugs, the blood, and something below that KEEPS what it takes.

entities.name: Umber hulk tunnels (old)
entities.note: Backstory, not an encounter. It ate this floor out and left generations ago.

entities.name: Two mimics (the table and the roast)
entities.note: Perception DC 16 / Arcana DC 14. Trigger on touching food or sitting.

entities.name: The hidden camp — safe long rest
entities.note: Genuinely safe. Let them take it.
